 he would vote

line-height:150%;text-align:left;mso-line-height-alt:25.5px;"><p style=3D"m=
argin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">Things really started to roll downhill after Se=
nator <strong>James
Lankford</strong> innocently asked Gabbard whether <strong>Edward Snowden</=
strong>, who leaked classified information revealing surveillance programs,=
 was a traitor. Gabbard said he broke the law. (True. He was <em>convicted<=
/em>.) She also said she would not advocate that he be pardoned=E2=80=94a n=
otable pivot from her previous position, when she and <strong>Matt Gaetz</s=
trong> introduced legislation to pardon him when they both served in the Ho=
use.=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0
</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">But Gabbard still seemed wishy=
-washy about Snowden, perhaps the most reviled character in the U.S. intell=
igence community. In fact, Gabbard was asked versions of Lankford=E2=80=99s=
 question no fewer than six times by senators from both parties=E2=80=94inc=
luding Democrat <strong>Michael Bennet</strong> of Colorado and Republican =
<strong>Todd Young </strong>of Indiana=E2=80=94and never said =E2=80=9Cyes=
=E2=80=9D or =E2=80=9Cno.=E2=80=9D I=E2=80=99m also told by two sources tha=
t she refused to clarify her position in
the committee=E2=80=99s closed session with her=E2=80=94even after one memb=
er helpfully walked her through how she <em>should</em> answer the Snowden =
questions. Meanwhile, Snowden himself weighed in on social media during the=
 hearings, not so helpfully, including by reposting one of his own earlier =
tweets: =E2=80=9CIf this is treason, what they call loyalty is a crime.=E2=
=80=9D</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"ma=
rgin:0;">Committee members are now concerned about her judgment, as well as=
 the message
she=E2=80=99s sending: Can she really lead the intel workforce when she app=
arently respects the most hated man in their field? Collins, who had become=
 relatively comfortable with Gabbard, is now rethinking her position. Gabba=
rd=E2=80=99s performance made a swing vote of Lankford, who had previously =
backed her publicly. And Young appears skeptical, too.=C2=A0</p></div>
